title: mentha × sativa var. subrotundifolia
intro: Mentha × sativa var. subrotundifolia, also known as wild peppermint, is a natural hybrid of peppermint (Mentha × piperita) and spearmint (Mentha spicata), characterized by its robust and compact growth. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in soothing digestive issues, relieving headaches, and calming respiratory problems due to its high menthol content. The health benefits of wild peppermint include reducing nausea, improving mental focus, and providing antioxidant properties, making it a popular herbal remedy. Wild peppermint is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and salves, allowing for various forms of consumption to suit individual needs.
